title: Git Fundamentals
description: A generic hands-on Git course covering repositories, staging, commits, history, ignores, branching, merging, conflicts, remotes, file reset, commit reset, revert, stash, and reflog recovery.

steps.title: Initialize a Repository
hints: Run git init.
Set local user.name and user.email so commits work in a training environment.

steps.title: Stage and Commit
hints: Use git status before and after git add.
Commit README.md and src/app.txt together.

steps.title: Inspect Diffs and History
hints: Use git diff before staging and git diff --staged after staging.
Finish with a commit that records the notes file.

steps.title: Ignore Generated Files
hints: Create .gitignore.
Use git check-ignore to verify ignored paths.

steps.title: Create and Work on a Branch
hints: Use git switch -c feature/profile-card.
Commit profile.md on the feature branch.

steps.title: Merge a Feature Branch
hints: Switch back to main.
Merge feature/profile-card into main.

steps.title: Resolve a Merge Conflict
hints: Create two branches that edit the same line in profile.md.
Resolve the conflict by keeping Role: Collaborative editor.

steps.title: Create and Push to a Remote
hints: Use a local bare repository as origin.
Push main with -u so the branch tracks origin/main.

steps.title: Fetch and Pull Remote Changes
hints: Clone the local remote into a sibling collaborator directory.
Push a collaborator commit, then fetch and pull it back here.

steps.title: Reset and Restore Files
hints: Use git restore --staged to unstage.
Use git restore to discard the working-tree edit.

steps.title: Revert a Commit Safely
hints: Make a bad commit.
Use git revert HEAD so history records the undo.

steps.title: Reset Local Commits
hints: Create a throwaway commit.
Use git reset --hard HEAD~1 only because this is local training work.

steps.title: Stash Interrupted Work
hints: Use git stash push -u -m "wip training note".
Make and commit a hotfix before popping the stash.

steps.title: Recover with Reflog
hints: Create a commit, reset it away, then find it in git reflog.
Recover it with git cherry-pick or git reset --hard.
